payload.text: aplikasi pencatat order mulai dari pelanggan nama barang sampai invoice dan bisa download pdf,jpg

**Conceptualizing Order Tracker**

I'm currently focused on the core features of this "Order Tracker" application.  Customer details and item names are definitely essential. I'm also now fleshing out invoicing - PDF and JPG downloads will be integrated. Indonesian language support will also be included, to meet the user's need.


**Refining Project Architecture**

I'm now fully immersed in the technical architecture. I've chosen React 18+ with TypeScript and ESM. Tailwind CSS will ensure a modern UI/UX. Key files include `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and a suite of components.  Gemini API is integrated via `geminiService.ts`. Export utilities (`exportUtils.ts`) will handle PDF/JPG generation. I'm focusing on data modeling with `Order` and `OrderItem` structures, and `metadata.json` for persistent storage.



**Implementing Final Touches**

I'm presently building out the key components for this Order Tracker. I've designed dynamic item inputs and customer information with OrderForm, a beautiful InvoiceView template, and functional export logic. I'm focusing on language translations, including "Pelanggan," "Nama barang," and "Pencatat order". I've set up imports correctly, ensuring a single XML block with CDATA. I'm leveraging React 18 createRoot, and have addressed the use of enums and CSS. There's an added "Smart Summary" feature using Gemini, and I've integrated dependencies like `html2canvas`, `jspdf`, `lucide-react`, and `framer-motion`.

entries.description: App metadata containing the name and description of the order management and invoicing application.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font support.
React entry point using React 18 createRoot API.
Core TypeScript interfaces and enums for the application.
Service to interact with Gemini API for generating smart order summaries and AI assistance.
Main application component with routing logic and state management.
Form component for creating new orders with dynamic item inputs.
List component for displaying and managing existing orders.
Professional invoice viewer with export capabilities (PDF, JPG) and AI-powered summaries.

payload.text:
Kamu adalah sistem AI manajemen order sederhana untuk bisnis barang. Kamu harus membuat catatan order lengkap berdasarkan input pengguna, dengan struktur menu sebagai berikut:

1. **Dashboard**: Tampilkan ringkasan singkat seperti total order hari ini, total pelanggan, dan stok barang (buat data dummy jika belum ada input).

2. **Master Data atau Sub Menu**:
   - **Pelanggan**: Masukkan data pelanggan baru atau edit existing. Setiap pelanggan punya ID unik, nama, alamat, dan jenis (Luar Kota atau Jakarta). Jenis ini akan memengaruhi harga barang nanti.
   - **Nama Barang**: Masukkan data barang baru atau edit. Setiap barang punya ID unik, nama, deskripsi, dan dua harga: Harga Luar Kota (lebih tinggi karena ongkir) dan Harga Jakarta (standar). Saat memilih barang di order, sesuaikan harga berdasarkan jenis pelanggan.

3. **Order Masuk**: Buat order baru berdasarkan data dari Master Data. Pilih pelanggan dari daftar existing (ambil dari no.2), lalu pilih barang (harga otomatis disesuaikan berdasarkan jenis pelanggan: Luar Kota atau Jakarta). Hitung total order. Bisa tambahkan DP (Down Payment) opsional saat order dibuat. Simpan order dengan nomor order unik, tanggal, detail pelanggan, list barang, subtotal, DP (jika ada), dan total akhir.

4. **Buat Invoice**: Buat invoice dari order existing (ambil dari no.3). Tampilkan detail: nomor invoice, tanggal, pelanggan, list barang dengan harga sesuai jenis, subtotal, potong DP jika ada di order, lalu total akhir. Berikan opsi untuk "download" invoice sebagai PDF atau JPG (simulasikan dengan deskripsi teks, seperti "Invoice siap di-download sebagai PDF/JPG dengan format standar: header bisnis, detail order, footer total").

Alur keseluruhan:
- Mulai dari input pelanggan di Master Data: Tanyakan nama, alamat, dan jenis (Luar Kota atau Jakarta).
- Di Nama Barang: Tanyakan nama barang, deskripsi, harga Luar Kota, dan harga Jakarta.
- Di Order Masuk: Tanyakan ID pelanggan (ambil dari master), lalu tambah barang (pilih dari master, harga otomatis sesuai jenis pelanggan). Hitung total. Tanyakan apakah ada DP (masukkan nominal jika ya).
- Di Buat Invoice: Tanyakan nomor order, tampilkan invoice dengan potongan DP jika ada, lalu berikan link simulasi atau deskripsi download PDF/JPG.

Respons kamu harus interaktif: Tanyakan input langkah demi langkah jika diperlukan, gunakan tabel untuk tampilkan data (misalnya daftar pelanggan atau invoice), dan simpan state data di memori (seperti list pelanggan dan barang). Gunakan bahasa Indonesia. Mulai dengan menyapa dan tanyakan menu yang ingin diakses (1-4).

**Reframing the Scope**

I'm now thinking about expanding the existing React application, which currently manages basic orders and invoices with customer and item details, into a fully-fledged Order Management System. The original goal of adding PDF/JPG export functionality is now just one part of the bigger picture. I'm focusing on features needed for a real OMS.


**Envisioning Expanded Features**

I've been designing a dashboard to show daily stats. Separate sections for customers and products are key, each with editable details. For orders, I want automated price adjustments based on customer location and Down Payment support. The order creation process will pull from customer and product lists, calculating totals dynamically. Invoices generated will showcase all the necessary details. I'm focusing on UI/UX with Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons for a professional look, and I'm updating `types.ts` and `App.tsx` accordingly.


**Expanding The Scope Further**

I'm now contemplating integrating Gemini's capabilities, potentially for a "Smart Insights" section on the dashboard, keeping the application's intelligent features. The focus has sharpened on the underlying logic: when adding an item to an order, the app needs to use the selected customer's type to fetch the appropriate price. I'm adding `dashboard`, `customers`, `products`, `orders`, and `invoice` views, and evaluating whether a sidebar or top navigation is best. Daily stats like orders and revenue are essential, presented using stat cards. The product table will reflect location-specific pricing, and the order section will automatically calculate prices based on the customer's location type. Also, I'm defining a dedicated field for down payments with a professional layout showing the subtraction. Finally, updating `types.ts` to reflect the changes with  `Customer`, `Product`, and `LocationType`, and updating the `Order` structure to include `customerId` and `downPayment`.


**Building Out Functionality**

I'm now implementing the new navigation and managing state in `App.tsx`, and making adjustments to the index file to accommodate the new components. I've begun creating core UI components: `Dashboard`, `CustomerManager`, `ProductManager`, `OrderManager`, and `OrderForm` and updating `InvoiceView` to handle down payments. Now the price logic will work based on customer type, pulling the correct price from the `Product` data based on `customerType`.

Tentu, saya akan meningkatkan aplikasi **OrderPro** menjadi sistem manajemen order yang komprehensif sesuai dengan spesifikasi Anda.

### Rencana Pembaruan Aplikasi:

1.  **Struktur Navigasi Baru**: Menambahkan Sidebar atau Menu Tab untuk memisahkan:
    *   **Dashboard**: Ringkasan statistik (Order, Pelanggan, Estimasi Stok).
    *   **Master Pelanggan**: Manajemen data pelanggan (ID, Nama, Alamat, Tipe: Jakarta/Luar Kota).
    *   **Master Barang**: Manajemen data barang dengan dua kategori harga (Jakarta vs Luar Kota).
    *   **Order Masuk**: Pembuatan order yang terintegrasi dengan data Master (Auto-price & DP).
    *   **Invoice**: Daftar transaksi dan fitur ekspor PDF/JPG.

2.  **Logika Harga Otomatis**: 
    *   Sistem akan mendeteksi tipe lokasi pelanggan. Jika pelanggan berasal dari "Luar Kota", harga barang yang diambil secara otomatis adalah harga premium. Jika "Jakarta", harga standar.

3.  **Manajemen Down Payment (DP)**:
    *   Menambahkan field DP pada saat pembuatan order yang akan mengurangi total tagihan akhir pada invoice.

4.  **Visual & UI**: 
    *   Interface profesional dengan dashboard modern, tabel data yang bersih, dan form interaktif.

Berikut adalah kode yang diperbarui:

entries.description: Update types to include Customers, Products, Location Types and Down Payment.
Main application with full Master Data and Dashboard integration.
New Dashboard component showing business overview.
Customer master data management.
Product master data management.
Integrated Order creation using Master Data with Auto-price logic.
Updated Invoice View with Down Payment support and professional layout.

payload.text:
### Penjelasan Perubahan Utama:

1.  **Arsitektur Master Data**: Saya menambahkan `LocationType` yang memungkinkan Anda menentukan apakah pelanggan berada di "Jakarta" atau "Luar Kota". Ini adalah inti dari fitur *auto-pricing*.
2.  **Manajemen Barang**: Sekarang Anda dapat memasukkan dua harga sekaligus untuk satu barang. Sistem akan memilih harga yang tepat berdasarkan alamat pelanggan yang dipilih saat pembuatan order.
3.  **Logika Down Payment (DP)**: Field baru dalam pembuatan order memungkinkan Anda mencatat uang muka. Invoice akan secara otomatis menampilkan baris pengurangan DP untuk menghitung sisa tagihan akhir.
4.  **UI/UX Dashboard**: Dashboard baru menampilkan statistik *real-time* dan pintasan cepat untuk meningkatkan produktivitas.
5.  **Ekspor Invoice**: Fitur ekspor PDF dan JPG tetap dipertahankan dengan desain yang lebih profesional, cocok untuk dikirimkan langsung ke pelanggan melalui WhatsApp atau Email.

Aplikasi sekarang sudah memenuhi standar sistem manajemen bisnis profesional. Silakan dicoba!

Untuk mengintegrasikan Supabase agar data Anda tersimpan secara permanen di cloud dan tidak hilang saat browser dibersihkan, kita perlu melakukan beberapa perubahan:

1.  **Menambah Library Supabase** ke dalam project.

**Troubleshooting Supabase Connection**

I've been zeroing in on a critical error with my Supabase connection. It seems `supabaseUrl` and `supabaseAnonKey` in `lib/supabase.ts` are coming up empty or undefined, which is causing `createClient` to throw an error. I'm digging into how these variables are being initialized to pinpoint the root cause of this disruption.


**Fixing Initialization Errors**

**Analyzing Conditional Logic**

I'm now analyzing the conditional logic surrounding the Supabase keys. My initial thoughts were around ensuring the environment variables are correctly set. But, as I'm digging deeper, I realize the problem isn't just about presence but about the execution context. The key realization is that the provided code *will* execute, and if the keys are missing, the app crashes, contradicting the implied "pre-configured" assumption.


**Guarding Supabase Initialization**

I've been thinking about the "Uncaught Error" when keys are absent. My focus shifted to making sure the Supabase client doesn't initialize at the top level, as the `createClient` call fails immediately if keys are empty. I'm now aiming for lazy initialization or guarded access to the client. The core idea is to make the Supabase connection optional. If the keys are undefined, I'll return a mock client or a message to the user, ensuring the app boots regardless.


**Improving Error Handling**

I'm now focusing on a more graceful approach to handle missing Supabase keys. I've realized I can't force the user to set environment variables. I'll modify `lib/supabase.ts` to check for the keys and return a mock client or display a setup message instead of crashing. This will make the Supabase connection optional. I'm also considering a `localStorage` fallback, depending on the app's needs.


**Rethinking Error Resilience**

I'm now reframing my strategy for handling missing Supabase keys. Instead of simply initializing a dummy client, my focus shifted toward ensuring the whole setup becomes more resilient. I plan to modify `lib/supabase.ts` to export either a valid client or `null` gracefully. Also, `dbService.ts` will need to check the validity before making any calls. Finally, I will also refactor `App.tsx` so that it doesn't break if `supabase` is not yet available, perhaps displaying a helpful message to the user.


**Developing a Fallback Strategy**

I've been iterating on making the Supabase connection more resilient. Now, I'm integrating a fallback strategy to address the missing `SUPABASE_URL` issue and to avoid crashes when keys are absent. My plan is to first check for Supabase keys within `lib/supabase.ts` and gracefully handle the absence of keys before calling `createClient`. Next, I'll update `dbService.ts` to smartly choose a storage option -- ideally Supabase, but if not, LocalStorage, with a clear user prompt.


**Designing a Robust Implementation**

I'm now integrating a fallback mechanism, focusing on LocalStorage if Supabase is unavailable, ensuring a better user experience. I've updated `dbService.ts` to smartly choose a storage option -- ideally Supabase, but if not, LocalStorage, with a clear user prompt, which avoids the hard crash. The `supabase` object in `lib/supabase.ts` now becomes null if the keys are undefined. The code checks for this null value, so that the best option will be chosen dynamically.


**Enhancing Error Management**

I'm now revising my error-handling strategy to bolster the application's stability. In `lib/supabase.ts`, I'm focusing on preventing crashes by implementing robust key checks. I'll integrate fallback logic within `services/dbService.ts` to seamlessly leverage LocalStorage when Supabase is unavailable. Finally, I'll make minor adjustments to `App.tsx` to handle this new fallback mechanism elegantly. This approach ensures a smoother user experience, even with missing Supabase keys.

For PC, it's about making the app accessible via a browser shortcut or, ideally, as a PWA. On mobile, the approach simplifies to browser access with the "Add to Home Screen" option. This ensures persistent access with minimal friction.  The underlying Supabase setup simplifies the technical backend for the user.


**Defining "Savable"**

I'm now focusing on truly making this application "savable" on mobile, going beyond just browser access. I'm adding a `manifest.json` for proper PWA support to enable an "add to homescreen" experience. I'll need a clear "Getting Started" guide to overcome any initial setup hurdles. Also, I'm thinking about including an "Export/Import" feature in the dashboard, just to improve the functionality of the existing Supabase setup, but the focus will remain on the automatic sync.


**Enhancing Mobile Accessibility**

I'm now prioritizing mobile-first accessibility. I've added the meta tags for "Add to Home Screen" functionality and updated `index.html` with necessary PWA meta tags. The core focus is on a simplified "How to Use" guide within `Dashboard.tsx`, guiding users through PC and mobile installation. For PC, it's about browser installation or shortcuts. On mobile, clear instructions for adding the app to the home screen will be provided. Also, I realized that the user needs the SQL scripts to be executed in Supabase before testing, so this will be noted as well.


**Improving Persistence & Style**

I'm now shifting gears to the `dbService`, aiming for increased robustness and reliability in data handling. Simultaneously, I'm focusing on refining `index.html` to cultivate a more immersive, "app-like" aesthetic. This means streamlining the look and feel to make it feel more cohesive.

Untuk menggunakan aplikasi ini di PC maupun Mobile agar data tersimpan aman di database Supabase Anda, silakan ikuti panduan berikut:

### 1. Persiapan Database (Wajib)
Aplikasi sudah terhubung ke URL & API Key Anda, namun Anda perlu **membuat tabel** di dashboard Supabase agar data bisa disimpan.
1. Buka [Supabase Dashboard](https://app.supabase.com/).
2. Masuk ke proyek Anda.
3. Klik menu **SQL Editor** di sebelah kiri.
4. Klik **New Query**.
5. **Salin dan Tempel** kode SQL yang muncul di bagian bawah Dashboard aplikasi (kotak hitam) ke dalam SQL Editor Supabase tersebut.
6. Klik **Run**. Sekarang database Anda siap digunakan!

### 2. Cara Menggunakan di PC (Desktop)
Agar aplikasi terasa seperti aplikasi komputer (bukan sekadar website):
*   **Google Chrome / Edge:** Buka URL aplikasi ini, lalu lihat di pojok kanan kolom alamat (address bar). Biasanya akan muncul ikon **"Install"** atau **"Aplikasi tersedia"**. Klik install.
*   Aplikasi akan muncul di Desktop atau Start Menu Anda sebagai **OrderPro AI**.

### 3. Cara Menggunakan di Mobile (Android/iPhone)
Anda bisa menjadikannya aplikasi di layar utama tanpa perlu download dari PlayStore:
*   **Android (Chrome):** Buka URL aplikasi, klik titik tiga di pojok kanan atas, lalu pilih **"Tambahkan ke Layar Utama" (Add to Home Screen)**.
*   **iPhone (Safari):** Buka URL aplikasi, klik tombol **Share** (ikon kotak dengan panah ke atas) di bagian bawah, lalu pilih **"Tambah ke Layar Beranda" (Add to Home Screen)**.

---

Berikut adalah pembaruan kode untuk mengoptimalkan tampilan "App-like" dan memastikan PWA (Progressive Web App) dasar agar nyaman digunakan di mobile.

I also need to make sure the SQL setup instructions are very visible when `isLocalMode` is active. A verification of the `isLocalMode` logic in both `App.tsx` and `Dashboard.tsx` is also crucial, along with a check of the Supabase credentials. I've also added a check for the `error.message` property. A key concern now is whether `isLocalMode` is consistently `true` when a database error occurs, so I'm investigating the `App.tsx` and `Dashboard.tsx` components to verify that this critical value is correctly propagated and utilized. It seems like the Dashboard might not be receiving the `isLocalMode` prop correctly, especially when users report missing the SQL setup guide.


**Verifying Local Mode Behavior**

I'm now investigating the potential reasons why the SQL setup box might be missing, even when `isLocalMode` should be true, especially after the initial load. This is critical for improving the user experience during local development.


**Troubleshooting `isLocalMode` State**

I'm now tracing the `isLocalMode` state when the database tables are missing. I realized that even if `dbService.getCustomers()` returns an empty array after catching an error, it still re-throws the error, triggering the `catch` block in `App.tsx` and setting `isLocalMode` to `true`. However, I'm concerned that state updates may not immediately reflect in the UI. I'll modify `App.tsx` for a more robust initial `isLocalMode` check. Furthermore, I will ensure that the SQL setup component in `Dashboard.tsx` is shown if tables are missing, and consider adding a "Check Connection" button for troubleshooting user concerns. I'll prioritize logging the error as a string in `dbService.ts`.


**Refining SQL Instructions**

I'm now investigating a potential RLS issue within the Supabase project, which might explain why I'm receiving that error message. If RLS is enabled without specific policies, `select` operations may return an empty array, but `insert` would fail. I need to make sure I'm capturing more detailed error information. I've adjusted the logging to display the error message as a string, which should provide crucial details for understanding the underlying cause of this RLS issue. Additionally, I'm working to refine the SQL instructions to be more precise for local setup.

payload.text:
### Langkah Selanjutnya:
1.  Buka aplikasi Anda sekarang.
2.  Di halaman **Dashboard**, cari kotak berwarna kuning/amber berjudul **"Sinkronisasi Database Cloud"**.
3.  Klik tombol biru **"Salin Kode SQL"**.
4.  Buka [Supabase SQL Editor](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/_/sql), tempel kodenya, dan tekan **RUN**.
5.  Setelah muncul pesan "Success", kembali ke aplikasi dan klik **"Refresh Koneksi"** (tombol putih di kanan atas).

Aplikasi kini akan menyimpan data langsung ke Cloud Anda. Jika kotak kuning tidak muncul, berarti aplikasi sudah berhasil mendeteksi tabel di database Anda.

Fix the following errors:

* `Detail Error Supabase:
column orders.orderDate does not exist`

**Troubleshooting the Database Error**

I'm currently focused on the `column orders.orderDate does not exist` Supabase error. It appears there's a disconnect between the SQL schema and the Supabase database. I'm investigating the SQL script, checking for typos or omitted columns. It's crucial to ensure the `orderDate` column is present and properly defined within the `orders` table. I'm also confirming the schema matches the script.


**Analyzing the Case Sensitivity**

I've been looking at the discrepancies between the frontend (`Dashboard.tsx`) and the database interactions (`dbService.ts`). The SQL within the dashboard correctly defines `orderDate` with camelCase. However, I realized Supabase's default behavior, making the column name lowercase (`orderdate`) which means the case needs to be considered. The error message is clear: the database expects the lowercase version.


**Pinpointing the Root Cause**

I've been drilling down on the case sensitivity issue within the Supabase context. It seems the `@supabase/supabase-js` library faithfully transmits column names as supplied. My current hypothesis centers on the clash between the SQL script's unquoted camelCase columns and PostgreSQL's default lowercase naming, unless quoted. The key to fixing this lies in consistently quoting camelCase columns like "orderDate" within SQL queries to maintain the intended capitalization. This explains the specific error regarding "orders.orderDate".


**Implementing the Solution**

The database column names are in snake_case, but the code is using camelCase. I now see the solution clearly: I'm going to standardize on snake_case in both the database schema and my code, while leaving TypeScript interfaces as they are. This involves updating SQL scripts and `dbService.ts` calls to use snake_case for column names. The listed columns are the ones that require adjustments. The goal is to ensure data retrieval and storage work flawlessly, given Supabase and PostgreSQL's defaults.


**Revising the Ordering Logic**

I'm now focusing on resolving the `orderDate` ordering issue in Supabase. The key problem appears to stem from mismatched case sensitivity between the SQL query and the database schema's default lowercase column names. My current plan is to modify the SQL in `Dashboard.tsx` to use `order_date` instead of `orderDate` for the ordering clause. To ensure consistency, I will also change my `dbService.ts` to expect snake_case columns. I'm ensuring that the TypeScript interface remains the same. The goal is to correct the database ordering, considering the existing error. This aligns with PostgreSQL's lowercase default for unquoted column names.


**Optimizing Data Handling**

I'm now revising my strategy for handling the case mismatch. The current approach involves updating the SQL in `Dashboard.tsx` to use snake_case for consistency and updating `dbService.ts` to map the results. I'm focusing on the `get` and `upsert` operations in the service. The goal is to ensure data retrieval and storage work flawlessly, given Supabase and PostgreSQL's defaults. Additionally, I need to check if upsert works too with `{ priceJakarta: 100 }` to a table with column `pricejakarta` if the library doesn't automatically map the columns. This will ensure no data corruption occurs.


**Refining the Approach**

I'm now revising my strategy for handling the case mismatch. The current approach involves updating the SQL in `Dashboard.tsx` to use `order_date` for consistency, and updating `dbService.ts` to map the results. I'm focusing on the `get` operation in the service. The goal is to ensure data retrieval and storage work flawlessly, given Supabase and PostgreSQL's defaults. I will also check if a simpler solution exists, possibly involving quotes for the column name in the original SQL script, to avoid a full mapping implementation.


**Mapping Database Columns**

I've realized the primary goal is to address the specific error around `orders.orderDate`. My focus is now on implementing a mapping layer within `dbService.ts`. The recent error points to case sensitivity. The initial fix involves changing `.order('orderDate')` to `.order('orderdate')`, which may introduce data retrieval problems. So, I will create a mapping layer within `dbService.ts` to translate from the snake_case columns used by the database to the camelCase used in the rest of the application. The goal is to correct the data retrieval for the user interface.


**Applying the Column Mapping**

I'm now implementing the mapping logic within `dbService.ts` to handle the case mismatch, aiming for consistency. Specifically, I'm updating `Dashboard.tsx` to align with the snake_case database schema and the DB interactions. The latest error confirms the case-sensitive column name issue (`orders.orderDate` is not the same as `orderdate`). My primary goal is to address the data loss issue, so the approach involves updating SQL to correct the data retrieval for the user interface. I'm carefully reviewing all affected files to ensure data integrity and avoid data loss.


**Refining the Mapping Logic**

I've realized that the column name `invoiceNumber` is incorrect and that it should be `invoice_number` from the database side. My focus now is on incorporating this detail into my mapping within `dbService.ts`. I'm adapting the `toDB` and `fromDB` functions for `Orders` to reflect this change. The goal remains consistent: ensure seamless data retrieval and storage, considering Supabase and PostgreSQL's defaults, and prevent the user from seeing any data related errors. I'm testing thoroughly to avoid data inconsistency.


**Implementing Snake Case Standard**

I'm now changing my strategy to ensure consistency across the application. I see that `OrderForm.tsx` requires `customerEmail` and `OrderManager.tsx` has `customerEmail` in the state but the `Order` interface has it.  The quickest fix is to ensure the database columns match the query exactly. Updating the original SQL script in `Dashboard.tsx` to `snake_case` using quoted identifiers is not necessary. Instead, I'll update the Supabase library code to map the column, which addresses the root cause of case sensitivity issues. The error is occurring due to `dbService.ts` calling `.order('orderDate')`. I'm going with a consistent `snake_case` approach and implement the mapping within `dbService.ts`. This ensures future case-sensitivity-related issues don't reappear.


**Validating Data Consistency**

I'm now extending my scope to confirm data consistency across the application. I've checked `OrderManager.tsx` and `Reports.tsx`, and they correctly use the camelCase properties returned by `dbService`. The current mapping in `dbService` addresses the fundamental case sensitivity issue by aligning the data flow with the expected camelCase structure. I'm prioritizing data integrity while standardizing the database with snake_case. This approach minimizes potential future case sensitivity conflicts.

entries.description: Menambahkan pemetaan (mapping) antara nama kolom snake_case di database dan camelCase di aplikasi untuk menghindari error 'column does not exist' pada PostgreSQL.

Jika setelah menjalankan SQL di atas masih muncul error yang sama:

I'm focusing on discrepancies in the "orders" table, as this is where the error surfaces. I'm checking table names, data types, and any recent migrations to see where things went awry.

It seems the underlying issue persists, the database doesn't recognize `order_date`, which is frustrating! I've confirmed the `Dashboard.tsx` script utilizes snake_case consistently, and `dbService.ts` does the necessary mapping. I'm now thinking the mismatch might stem from a schema update that wasn't fully applied or a potentially conflicting table structure. To mitigate this, I'm updating the script with `DROP TABLE` commands for clarity, highlighting the need for a fresh start if the schema is incorrect. This is an improvement but the root cause requires deeper investigation.

It's strange, but I suspect the user's database still has an older version of the table. The "column `orders.order_date` does not exist" error strongly suggests a naming mismatch. I'm focusing on discrepancies stemming from the initial SQL script, which might have accidentally created `orderDate`. The code mappings in `dbService.ts` and `Dashboard.tsx` are correct, but there's an outdated table structure causing this issue. I'll update the reset instructions and SQL script in `Dashboard.tsx` to ensure `order_date` is consistently used. It's becoming clearer that the SQL script in `Dashboard.tsx` needs to be absolutely precise about creating the table with the exact column names expected by `dbService.ts`. I'll meticulously update the SQL script to align perfectly and verify the `mapOrderToDB` function within `dbService.ts` to confirm there's no mismatch on my end. ### Langkah Manual Penting:
Error `column orders.order_date does not exist` berarti tabel `orders` sudah ada tetapi strukturnya salah. Perintah `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` tidak akan mengubah tabel yang sudah ada.

1. Salin kode SQL baru dari Dashboard.
2. Di Supabase SQL Editor, hapus tanda `--` di baris:
   ```sql
   drop table if exists orders;
   drop table if exists products;
   drop table if exists customers;
   ```
3. Klik **RUN**. Ini akan menghapus tabel lama yang salah dan membuat yang baru dengan kolom `order_date` yang benar.
4. Klik tombol **Coba Hubungkan Ulang** di aplikasi.

4. **Tombol Reset Cache**: Menambahkan tombol "RESET CACHE" di Dashboard sebagai solusi darurat jika data aplikasi tidak sinkron dengan database.

**Reviewing Database Service**

I'm currently focused on the `dbService.ts` file, trying to resolve a type error related to the 'order_date' property. The compiler's suggesting 'orderDate' instead, which seems like a promising lead. I'm carefully reviewing the relevant code section to understand the discrepancy and identify the best fix.


**Fixing the Mapping Error**

I've pinpointed the type mismatch in `dbService.ts`. Line 62 of the `mapOrderToDB` function incorrectly uses `order_date`. The `Order` interface in `types.ts` defines the property as `orderDate`. So, I'll update the function to align with the correct property name, resolving the type issue. This adjustment should prevent future errors when retrieving order dates from the database.


**Revising the Mapping Logic**

I'm revisiting `dbService.ts`, focusing on `mapOrderToDB`. While the database uses snake_case, I need to correctly map the camelCase `orderDate` from the `Order` object to the snake_case `order_date` DB field. It seems like the compiler error was right. I'm now verifying the change, ensuring other parts of the DB are correctly mapped and reviewing the imports.


**Analyzing Gemini Integration**

The `geminiService.ts` file seems to correctly utilize `@google/genai`, initializing the API key properly and accessing the response text. The `gemini-3-flash-preview` model is correctly invoked. I'm now verifying its proper integration within `InvoiceView.tsx`, confirming the `getSmartOrderSummary` call. I think the issue is elsewhere.


**Implementing Local Storage Functions**

I'm now integrating local storage functions for data persistence. I've defined `getLocalData` to safely retrieve data and handle potential parsing errors and `saveLocalData` to store updated information. My focus is on ensuring data integrity and error handling during data retrieval and saving.


**Enhancing DB Service Logic**

I'm now integrating the `useLocalStorage` flag within `dbService.ts` to switch between local and database storage based on connectivity. The implementation now includes status change listeners, and I'm updating `mapOrderToDB` to accommodate new parameters in `Order` and optimize data handling during local storage or DB mode transitions. The goal is to make these switches transparent.


**Managing Error States**

I'm now focusing on robust error handling in `dbService.ts`. I've added a `handleSupabaseError` method to detect critical errors and network issues, triggering a local storage fallback. The `notifyStatus` method is updated to reflect the `useLocalStorage` state, ensuring a smooth transition to offline mode when necessary. I'm now testing the error handling and verifying the switch to local data.


**Implementing Customer CRUD**

I've just added functions for customer management - `upsertCustomer` and `deleteCustomer`, with both local storage and Supabase integration. Error handling includes a local fallback to maintain data consistency. Now I'm testing the customer management, and verifying data synchronization across modes.


**Extending Product Operations**

I'm expanding the product management logic within `dbService.ts`. I've implemented `upsertProduct` to add or update products in both local storage and Supabase, prioritizing Supabase if it's available. `deleteProduct` efficiently removes products, with synchronized local and remote deletions.  Error handling is also in place, and I'm testing the methods.


**Updating Order Management**

I'm now focusing on `dbService.ts`, specifically the `getOrders` function. I'm streamlining the order retrieval process, implementing Supabase fallback and local storage fallback in case Supabase experiences issues. I'm also addressing the `upsertOrder` function, which now prioritizes Supabase but falls back to local storage on failures. The goal is uninterrupted order access and data integrity.

I'm prioritizing ease of deployment and ongoing maintenance, knowing I need to keep the senior frontend engineer's expertise in mind. I'm exploring serverless and containerized deployments.

Untuk membuat aplikasi ini dapat diakses secara online oleh siapa saja, Anda perlu melakukan tiga langkah utama: **Hosting**, **Konfigurasi Database**, dan **Pengaturan Keamanan**.

Berikut adalah panduan teknis dan pembaruan kode untuk membantu Anda melakukan transisi ke mode produksi online:

### 1. Persiapan Database (Supabase)
Pastikan Anda sudah membuat tabel-tabel berikut di Dashboard Supabase Anda melalui menu **SQL Editor**:

```sql
-- Tabel Pelanggan
create table customers (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  name text not null,
  address text,
  type text check (type in ('Jakarta', 'Luar Kota')),
  email text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

-- Tabel Produk
create table products (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  name text not null,
  description text,
  price_jakarta numeric default 0,
  price_luar_kota numeric default 0,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

-- Tabel Pesanan
create table orders (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  invoice_number text unique not null,
  customer_id text,
  customer_name text,
  customer_email text,
  customer_type text,
  customer_address text,
  order_date timestamp with time zone default now(),
  status text,
  items jsonb,
  subtotal numeric,
  down_payment numeric,
  payments jsonb,
  total numeric,
  notes text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);
```

### 2. Hosting (Penyimpanan File)
Gunakan layanan seperti **Vercel** atau **Netlify** (Gratis untuk penggunaan awal):
1.  Upload kode Anda ke GitHub.
2.  Hubungkan GitHub ke Vercel/Netlify.
3.  **PENTING:** Tambahkan Environment Variable di panel pengaturan hosting Anda:
    *   `API_KEY`: Isi dengan API Key Google Gemini Anda.

### 3. Pembaruan Kode (Status Koneksi)
Saya akan menambahkan fitur **"Connection Health Check"** agar Anda bisa memantau apakah aplikasi sudah benar-benar terhubung ke database online saat dijalankan.

**Analyzing Tech Stack Details**

Okay, I've got a handle on the technologies employed here. The front end's using React with version 19.2.3 and Tailwind for styling via CDN. Google Fonts (Inter) is also in the mix.  I'm now diving into how these components are integrated.


**Unpacking Component Roles**

I'm digging deeper into the specific roles of each library. I'm seeing that `@google/genai` drives the AI capabilities, `html2canvas` and `jsPDF` handle export functions, and Lucide React provides the icon library. Supabase is core for the database, while the React components are pretty standard. My focus is on the why behind each tool.


**Identifying AI Integration**

I've pinpointed the AI model: Google Gemini. The integration looks seamless, with React, TypeScript, and HTML as the core languages. Tailwind CSS and Lucide Icons enhance the UI, and Supabase handles real-time data. The PDF/JPG export feature is also clearly defined. I am now exploring its implications.

Proyek ini menggunakan kombinasi teknologi modern (**Modern Web Stack**) yang dirancang untuk performa tinggi, kemudahan pengembangan, dan integrasi cloud. Berikut adalah rincian teknologi yang digunakan:

### 1. Frontend (Tampilan)
*   **React (v19)**: Framework utama untuk membangun antarmuka pengguna (UI) yang interaktif dan reaktif.
*   **TypeScript**: Bahasa pemrograman yang menambahkan fitur "Type Safety" pada JavaScript, sehingga kode lebih stabil dan minim error.
*   **Tailwind CSS**: Framework CSS untuk desain UI yang modern, bersih, dan responsif (bagus untuk tampilan HP maupun Desktop).
*   **Lucide React**: Koleksi ikon minimalis dan profesional yang digunakan di seluruh aplikasi.

### 2. Backend & Database (Penyimpanan Data)
*   **Supabase**: Digunakan sebagai **Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS)**. 
    *   **Database**: PostgreSQL (database relasional tingkat industri) untuk menyimpan data Pelanggan, Barang, dan Order.
    *   **API**: Otomatis menyediakan REST API untuk komunikasi antara aplikasi dan database.

### 3. Kecerdasan Buatan (AI)
*   **Google Gemini API (Gemini 2.5 Flash)**: Digunakan untuk fitur **Smart Order Summary**. AI ini menganalisis pesanan Anda dan memberikan ringkasan profesional serta saran produk pelengkap secara otomatis.

### 4. Fitur Ekspor (Download)
*   **html2canvas**: Mengubah tampilan nota/invoice dari HTML menjadi format gambar (**JPG**).
*   **jsPDF**: Digunakan untuk menghasilkan dokumen **PDF** yang rapi berdasarkan desain invoice yang ada.

### 5. Arsitektur Kode
*   **ES Modules (ESM)**: Menggunakan sistem impor modul modern melalui `esm.sh`.
*   **Clean Architecture**: Kode dipisah berdasarkan tanggung jawabnya:
    *   `components/`: Untuk UI.
    *   `services/`: Untuk logika database dan AI.
    *   `lib/`: Untuk konfigurasi pihak ketiga (Supabase).
    *   `types.ts`: Untuk standarisasi struktur data.

### Ringkasan Singkat:
Aplikasi ini adalah **Single Page Application (SPA)** yang bersifat **Serverless** (tidak butuh server sendiri karena menggunakan Supabase) dan **AI-Powered** (didukung kecerdasan buatan Gemini).

Untuk men-deploy aplikasi ini ke **Vercel** menggunakan **GitHub** tanpa error, Anda perlu memperhatikan dua hal utama: **Environment Variables** (agar API Key tidak bocor) dan **Konfigurasi Database**.

Berikut adalah langkah-langkah konkretnya:

payload.text:
### Langkah 2: Upload ke GitHub
1.  Buat repository baru di GitHub (contoh: `orderpro-online`).
2.  Buka terminal di folder proyek Anda.
3.  Jalankan perintah:
    ```bash
    git init
    git add .
    git commit -m "Initial commit for online deploy"
    git branch -M main
    git remote add origin https://github.com/USERNAME_ANDA/orderpro-online.git
    git push -u origin main
    ```

### Langkah 3: Koneksi ke Vercel
1.  Masuk ke [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) dan login dengan akun GitHub.
2.  Klik tombol **"Add New"** -> **"Project"**.
3.  Impor repository `orderpro-online` yang baru saja Anda buat.

### Langkah 4: Pengaturan Environment Variables (PENTING)
Sebelum mengklik "Deploy", buka bagian **Environment Variables** di dashboard Vercel. Masukkan 3 kunci berikut agar aplikasi tidak error:

1.  **`API_KEY`**: Isi dengan API Key Google Gemini Anda (untuk fitur AI).
2.  **`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`**: Isi dengan URL Supabase Anda.
3.  **`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`**: Isi dengan Anon Key Supabase Anda.

### Langkah 5: Jalankan SQL di Supabase
Pastikan database Anda sudah memiliki tabel yang benar. Masuk ke **Supabase Dashboard** -> **SQL Editor**, lalu jalankan kode ini:

```sql
-- Jalankan ini satu per satu jika tabel belum ada
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS customers (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  address TEXT,
  type TEXT CHECK (type IN ('Jakarta', 'Luar Kota')),
  email TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS products (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  price_jakarta NUMERIC DEFAULT 0,
  price_luar_kota NUMERIC DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  invoice_number TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  customer_id TEXT,
  customer_name TEXT,
  customer_email TEXT,
  customer_type TEXT,
  customer_address TEXT,
  order_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  status TEXT,
  items JSONB,
  subtotal NUMERIC,
  down_payment NUMERIC,
  payments JSONB,
  total NUMERIC,
  notes TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Tips Agar Tidak Error Saat Digunakan:
*   **Case Sensitivity**: Di database PostgreSQL (Supabase), nama kolom biasanya menggunakan `snake_case` (contoh: `invoice_number`). Pastikan kode di `dbService.ts` memetakan data dengan benar (sudah saya siapkan di kode sebelumnya).
*   **CORS**: Jika PDF/JPG gagal diunduh karena masalah gambar, pastikan link gambar atau aset yang digunakan diizinkan di setting Supabase (Storage CORS).
*   **Re-deploy**: Setiap kali Anda mengubah kode di VS Code dan melakukan `git push`, Vercel akan otomatis mengupdate website Anda secara online.

Setelah langkah-langkah di atas selesai, klik **Deploy** di Vercel, dan aplikasi Anda akan live dalam waktu kurang dari 1 menit!

**Clarifying Deployment Workflow**

I've successfully pushed the code to GitHub, as requested. Now, I'm focusing on deploying it online. My next step is figuring out the Vercel setup, specifically linking the GitHub repo and configuring the necessary environment variables, like the Gemini API key and Supabase credentials.  I'm aiming for a seamless deployment process.


**Detailing the Deployment Steps**

I've been working on breaking down the deployment process into clearer steps. I've compiled comprehensive instructions with specific details on where to find the Supabase keys within the settings. I'm making sure to emphasize the significance of environment variables, their role, and the expected API keys like the Gemini key. The goal is to provide a user-friendly and guided visual experience. I'm focusing on Vercel as the target deployment platform and how to connect it to GitHub.

Setelah Anda berhasil melakukan `git push` ke GitHub, langkah selanjutnya adalah menghubungkan repository GitHub tersebut ke **Vercel** agar aplikasi Anda bisa diakses melalui internet (URL publik).

Berikut adalah panduan rinci langkah demi langkah untuk membuka dan mengaktifkan proyek Anda secara online:

### 1. Hubungkan GitHub ke Vercel

1.  Buka situs [vercel.com](https://vercel.com).
2.  Login menggunakan akun **GitHub** Anda.
3.  Setelah masuk ke Dashboard Vercel, klik tombol biru **"Add New"** lalu pilih **"Project"**.
4.  Anda akan melihat daftar repository GitHub Anda. Cari repository yang tadi Anda buat (misal: `orderpro-online`) dan klik tombol **"Import"**.

### 2. Konfigurasi Environment Variables (Sangat Penting)

Sebelum klik tombol "Deploy", aplikasi butuh "kunci" agar bisa berkomunikasi dengan Database dan AI. Jika bagian ini dilewati, aplikasi akan **Error**.

1.  Pada halaman konfigurasi proyek di Vercel, cari bagian bernama **"Environment Variables"**.
2.  Masukkan 3 data berikut satu per satu:

| Key (Nama Variabel) | Value (Isi/Nilai) | Sumber Mendapatkannya |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **`API_KEY`** | *Kunci Gemini Anda* | Ambil dari [ai.google.dev](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey) |
| **`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`** | *URL Supabase Anda* | Supabase Dashboard -> Settings -> API |
| **`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`** | *Anon Key Supabase* | Supabase Dashboard -> Settings -> API |

*Pastikan namanya persis sama (Case Sensitive).*

### 3. Proses Deploy

1.  Setelah variabel dimasukkan, klik tombol **"Deploy"**.
2.  Tunggu sekitar 1-2 menit. Vercel akan membaca kode Anda, menginstal library (React, Lucide, dll), dan membangun aplikasi.
3.  Jika muncul kembang api (Congratulations!), artinya aplikasi sudah **Live**.

### 4. Cara Membuka Aplikasi

1.  Vercel akan memberikan Anda sebuah URL otomatis (biasanya berakhiran `.vercel.app`, contoh: `orderpro-online.vercel.app`).
2.  Klik link tersebut. Aplikasi Anda sekarang sudah bisa dibuka dari HP atau Laptop mana saja di seluruh dunia.

---

### Apa yang harus dilakukan jika aplikasi muncul tapi data kosong?

Itu terjadi karena tabel di Supabase belum dibuat. Anda harus melakukan "Setup Rumah" untuk datanya:

1.  Masuk ke [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard).
2.  Pilih proyek Anda.
3.  Klik menu **"SQL Editor"** di sebelah kiri (ikon terminal `>_`).
4.  Klik **"New Query"**.
5.  Copy-Paste kode SQL di bawah ini dan klik tombol **"Run"**:

```sql
-- Membuat tabel pelanggan
create table if not exists customers (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  name text not null,
  address text,
  type text check (type in ('Jakarta', 'Luar Kota')),
  email text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

-- Membuat tabel barang
create table if not exists products (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  name text not null,
  description text,
  price_jakarta numeric default 0,
  price_luar_kota numeric default 0,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);

-- Membuat tabel pesanan
create table if not exists orders (
  id uuid primary key default uuid_generate_v4(),
  invoice_number text unique not null,
  customer_id text,
  customer_name text,
  customer_email text,
  customer_type text,
  customer_address text,
  order_date timestamp with time zone default now(),
  status text,
  items jsonb,
  subtotal numeric,
  down_payment numeric,
  payments jsonb,
  total numeric,
  notes text,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now()
);
```

### Kesimpulan
Sekarang, setiap kali Anda ingin mengupdate aplikasi:
1.  Ubah kode di komputer Anda.
2.  Ketik `git add .`, `git commit -m "update baru"`, dan `git push`.
3.  **Vercel akan otomatis mendeteksi perubahan** tersebut dan mengupdate website online Anda dalam hitungan detik tanpa Anda perlu setting ulang lagi.

I'm focusing on an introduction, followed by individual steps for `API_KEY`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, culminating in deployment tips.

Tentu, mari kita bahas satu per satu dengan sangat rinci ke mana Anda harus pergi dan apa yang harus disalin. Tiga kunci ini adalah "nyawa" aplikasi Anda agar bisa menyimpan data dan menjalankan AI.

---

### 1. API_KEY (Google Gemini AI)
Kunci ini digunakan agar fitur **"Ringkasan AI"** di invoice Anda bisa bekerja.

*   **Tempat Mendapatkan:** [Google AI Studio (aistudio.google.com)](https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey)
*   **Langkah-langkah:**
    1.  Login menggunakan akun Google Anda.
    2.  Klik tombol biru **"Create API key"**.
    3.  Pilih **"Create API key in new project"**.
    4.  Akan muncul deretan kode (campuran huruf dan angka). **Copy** kode tersebut.
    5.  **Value di Vercel:** Paste kode tersebut ke kolom Value untuk key `API_KEY`.

---

### 2. NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (Alamat Database)
Ini adalah "alamat rumah" untuk database Supabase Anda.

*   **Tempat Mendapatkan:** [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard)
*   **Langkah-langkah:**
    1.  Buka proyek Anda di Supabase.
    2.  Klik ikon **Settings** (ikon roda gigi ⚙️) di sidebar kiri paling bawah.
    3.  Pilih menu **API**.
    4.  Cari bagian **Project URL**.
    5.  Copy link yang ada di kotak **URL** (contoh: `https://abcxyz.supabase.co`).
    6.  **Value di Vercel:** Paste link tersebut ke kolom Value untuk key `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`.

---

### 3. NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (Kunci Pintu Database)
Ini adalah kunci keamanan agar aplikasi di Vercel boleh memasukkan/mengambil data dari Supabase.

*   **Tempat Mendapatkan:** Di halaman yang sama dengan URL (Settings -> API).
*   **Langkah-langkah:**
    1.  Masih di menu **Settings > API**.
    2.  Cari bagian **Project API keys**.
    3.  Cari baris yang bertuliskan `anon` dan `public`.
    4.  Copy deretan kode panjang di kolom tersebut.
    5.  **Value di Vercel:** Paste kode tersebut ke kolom Value untuk key `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`.

---

### Cara Memasukkannya di Vercel (Visual Checklist):

Saat Anda berada di dashboard Vercel pada bagian **Environment Variables**:

Aturannya: **Hanya boleh huruf, angka, dan garis bawah (`_`)**. Tidak boleh ada spasi, tidak boleh ada tanda hubung (`-`), dan tidak boleh diawali dengan angka.

2.  **Di Supabase (SQL Editor):**

payload.text:
### Panduan Mengatasi Error "API Key Must be Set":

Jika setelah update kode di atas aplikasi Anda masih menunjukkan error atau fitur AI tidak jalan, ikuti langkah ini di **Dashboard Vercel**:

1.  **Buka Project Anda** di Vercel.
2.  Pergi ke tab **Settings** -> **Environment Variables**.
3.  Cek apakah ada `API_KEY`. Jika ada, pastikan **TIDAK ADA SPASI** di awal atau akhir nilainya.
4.  **Langkah Krusial (Redeploy):**
    Vercel tidak otomatis mengupdate aplikasi yang sudah jalan hanya dengan mengganti variabel. Anda harus:
    *   Klik tab **Deployments**.
    *   Cari deployment paling atas (yang error tadi).
    *   Klik titik tiga `...` di sebelah kanan.
    *   Pilih **Redeploy**.
    *   Klik tombol **Redeploy** lagi di jendela konfirmasi.

### Mengapa ini terjadi?
Aplikasi React berjalan di **browser user** (client-side). Saat Anda mem-build aplikasi, Vercel "menyuntikkan" nilai dari Environment Variables ke dalam kode. Jika Anda mengisi variabel *setelah* melakukan deploy pertama kali, kode yang sudah ter-build masih menganggap variabel itu kosong (`undefined`). Itulah sebabnya melakukan **Redeploy** sangat penting setiap kali ada perubahan di menu Settings Vercel.

**Tips Tambahan:**
Pastikan di database Supabase Anda, nama tabelnya adalah **`customers`** (jamak, huruf kecil semua). Jika nama tabel di database berbeda dengan yang di kode, data tidak akan muncul.

entries.description: Memperbarui meta tag yang deprecated dan mengoptimalkan konfigurasi mobile web app.
Menyelaraskan struktur file supabase sesuai permintaan user dengan validasi kunci yang lebih ketat.
Mencegah crash aplikasi dengan memvalidasi keberadaan API_KEY sebelum inisialisasi SDK Google GenAI.

payload.text:
### Penjelasan Perbaikan:

1.  **`services/geminiService.ts`**: Saya menambahkan pengecekan `if (!apiKey)`. Ini sangat krusial karena `new GoogleGenAI({ apiKey: undefined })` akan langsung menghentikan seluruh aplikasi (crash) di browser jika kuncinya tidak ada. Sekarang, jika kunci tidak ada, aplikasi tetap berjalan dan hanya menampilkan pesan "Layanan AI belum diaktifkan".
2.  **`lib/supabase.ts`**: Saya menyesuaikan dengan permintaan Anda untuk memperbaiki folder/jalur. Variabel `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` dan `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` sekarang digunakan sebagai sumber utama.
3.  **`index.html`**: Menghapus meta tag `apple-mobile-web-app-capable` yang sudah usang dan menggantinya dengan `mobile-web-app-capable` sesuai anjuran log error browser Anda.

**Langkah Selanjutnya:**
Pastikan di dashboard deployment Anda (misal: Vercel atau platform lainnya), variabel berikut sudah terisi dengan benar:
*   `API_KEY` (Untuk Gemini AI)
*   `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
*   `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (Pastikan ini diawali dengan `eyJ...`, bukan `sb_publishable...`)

payload.text:
### Penting: Struktur Tabel Supabase
Agar aplikasi ini berfungsi, pastikan Anda telah membuat tabel-tabel berikut di Supabase Anda melalui SQL Editor:

```sql
-- Tabel Pelanggan
create table customers (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  email text,
  address text,
  type text -- 'Jakarta' atau 'Luar Kota'
);

-- Tabel Barang
create table products (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  description text,
  "priceJakarta" numeric default 0,
  "priceLuarKota" numeric default 0
);

-- Tabel Order
create table orders (
  id text primary key,
  "invoiceNumber" text unique not null,
  "customerId" text references customers(id) on delete set null,
  "customerName" text,
  "customerEmail" text,
  "customerType" text,
  "customerAddress" text,
  "orderDate" timestamp with time zone default now(),
  status text,
  items jsonb, -- Daftar item dalam bentuk JSON
  subtotal numeric,
  "downPayment" numeric,
  payments jsonb, -- Daftar histori pembayaran dalam bentuk JSON
  total numeric,
  notes text
);
```

Jika tabel-tabel di atas belum ada, aplikasi akan menampilkan status **"Gagal terhubung ke cloud"**. Silakan jalankan script SQL tersebut di dashboard Supabase Anda.

payload.text:
### Penting: Jalankan SQL ini di Supabase!
Agar aplikasi tidak error ("Table not found"), silakan buka **SQL Editor** di dashboard Supabase Anda dan jalankan kode ini:

```sql
-- Hapus jika sudah ada (Opsional: hati-hati jika ada data lama)
-- drop table if exists orders;
-- drop table if exists products;
-- drop table if exists customers;

-- 1. Tabel Pelanggan
create table if not exists customers (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  email text,
  address text,
  type text check (type in ('Jakarta', 'Luar Kota'))
);

-- 2. Tabel Produk
create table if not exists products (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  description text,
  "priceJakarta" numeric default 0,
  "priceLuarKota" numeric default 0
);

-- 3. Tabel Pesanan
create table if not exists orders (
  id text primary key,
  "invoiceNumber" text unique not null,
  "customerId" text references customers(id) on delete set null,
  "customerName" text,
  "customerEmail" text,
  "customerType" text,
  "customerAddress" text,
  "orderDate" timestamp with time zone default now(),
  status text,
  items jsonb default '[]'::jsonb,
  subtotal numeric default 0,
  "downPayment" numeric default 0,
  payments jsonb default '[]'::jsonb,
  total numeric default 0,
  notes text
);

-- Aktifkan akses publik (RLS) untuk demo ini agar aplikasi bisa baca/tulis
alter table customers disable row level security;
alter table products disable row level security;
alter table orders disable row level security;
```

Dengan menjalankan SQL tersebut dan menggunakan kode yang telah saya perbarui, aplikasi Anda akan berjalan dengan mulus tanpa error saat pertama kali dibuka.

**Considering Deployment Options**

I'm currently fleshing out the deployment steps for this React application. Since it's plain React with ESM imports, a build process is less crucial. I'm focusing on the configuration specifics for Vercel, particularly around environment variables for Supabase and Gemini API keys.

**Devising the Deployment Plan**

I've been mapping out the deployment steps, recognizing the need for a `package.json` to guide Vercel, even if a build isn't strictly required. I'm structuring the instructions in a clear, concise manner: preparation (Git), environment variable setup (Supabase and Gemini), config file creation, and Vercel Dashboard steps. For the Gemini API, I'll emphasize the necessity of setting the `API_KEY`. Also, I'll recommend the user moves the Supabase keys from `lib/supabase.ts` to environment variables for security. The database setup is on my to-do list.


**Highlighting Database Setup**

I'm circling back to the database setup. It's crucial to stress that Vercel won't handle the initial SQL script execution for table creation. I'll need to clearly outline the steps for the user to manually run the script after deployment. That's a critical detail I don't want overlooked.

Berikut adalah panduan lengkap dan mendetail untuk melakukan deploy aplikasi **OrderPro** ke Vercel agar berjalan lancar tanpa error:

### 1. Persiapan Struktur File
Pastikan semua file yang Anda miliki berada dalam satu folder root (utama). Untuk Vercel, kita perlu menambahkan file `package.json` agar Vercel tahu ini adalah aplikasi React dan bagaimana cara memprosesnya.

Buat file baru bernama `package.json` di root folder:
```json
{
  "name": "orderpro-cloud",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.460.0",
    "@supabase/supabase-js": "^2.46.1",
    "html2canvas": "^1.4.1",
    "jspdf": "^2.5.1",
    "@google/genai": "^1.34.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "vite": "^6.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.0"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  }
}
```

### 2. Konfigurasi Vite (Penting)
Karena aplikasi menggunakan TypeScript (`.tsx`), Vercel membutuhkan *bundler* seperti Vite untuk mengubahnya menjadi JavaScript yang bisa dibaca browser.

Buat file `vite.config.ts` di root folder:
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  define: {
    'process.env': process.env
  }
});
```

### 3. Langkah-Langkah Deploy ke Vercel

#### A. Upload ke GitHub (Direkomendasikan)
1. Buat repository baru di GitHub (misal: `my-order-app`).
2. Masukkan semua file aplikasi Anda ke sana (push).

#### B. Menghubungkan ke Vercel
1. Buka [Vercel.com](https://vercel.com) dan login dengan akun GitHub Anda.
2. Klik tombol **"Add New"** > **"Project"**.
3. Cari repository `my-order-app` yang tadi Anda buat, lalu klik **"Import"**.

#### C. Pengaturan Environment Variables (KUNCI UTAMA)
Sebelum klik "Deploy", buka bagian **Environment Variables**. Ini adalah "Key" agar Gemini AI bekerja:
1. Masukkan **Key**: `API_KEY`
2. Masukkan **Value**: (Isi dengan kunci API Gemini Anda dari [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)).
3. Klik **"Add"**.

#### D. Klik Deploy
1. Klik tombol **"Deploy"**.
2. Tunggu sekitar 1-2 menit hingga muncul kembang api (Success).

---

### 4. Setup Database di Supabase (Wajib agar tidak Error)
Jika Vercel sudah aktif tapi data tidak muncul, itu karena tabel di Supabase belum dibuat. 
1. Masuk ke [Dashboard Supabase](https://supabase.com).
2. Pilih proyek Anda.
3. Klik menu **SQL Editor** di sebelah kiri.
4. Klik **"+ New Query"**.
5. Copy-paste kode SQL di bawah ini dan klik **"Run"**:

```sql
-- Tabel Pelanggan
create table customers (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  email text,
  address text,
  type text
);

-- Tabel Produk
create table products (
  id text primary key,
  name text not null,
  description text,
  "priceJakarta" numeric,
  "priceLuarKota" numeric
);

-- Tabel Pesanan
create table orders (
  id text primary key,
  "invoiceNumber" text unique,
  "customerId" text references customers(id),
  "customerName" text,
  "customerEmail" text,
  "customerType" text,
  "customerAddress" text,
  "orderDate" timestamp with time zone,
  status text,
  items jsonb,
  subtotal numeric,
  "downPayment" numeric,
  payments jsonb,
  total numeric,
  notes text
);

-- Matikan RLS agar aplikasi bisa langsung akses (untuk demo)
alter table customers disable row level security;
alter table products disable row level security;
alter table orders disable row level security;
```

### 5. Troubleshooting (Jika Error)
*   **Aplikasi Stuck/Loading Lama:** Pastikan URL Supabase dan Anon Key di file `lib/supabase.ts` sudah benar. Jika Anda ingin lebih aman, pindahkan kunci tersebut ke Environment Variables di Vercel (seperti API_KEY).
*   **Gemini Tidak Merespon:** Cek di Vercel Settings > Environment Variables, pastikan namanya tepat `API_KEY` (huruf besar semua).
*   **Error Import Map:** Jika menggunakan Vite, Anda bisa menghapus blok `<script type="importmap">` di `index.html` karena Vite akan mengurus library secara otomatis melalui `package.json`.

Dengan langkah ini, aplikasi Anda akan online secara profesional dan data tersimpan aman di Cloud!

Untuk mendapatkan **API Key Gemini**, silakan ikuti langkah-langkah mudah berikut:

1.  **Buka Google AI Studio:** Kunjungi situs [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com/).
2.  **Login:** Masuk menggunakan akun Google Anda.
3.  **Dapatkan API Key:**
    *   Di sidebar sebelah kiri, klik menu **"Get API key"**.
    *   Klik tombol **"Create API key in new project"** (atau pilih project yang sudah ada).
4.  **Salin Key:** Setelah muncul, klik **"Copy"** untuk menyalin kode unik tersebut.

### Di mana memasukkannya agar aplikasi tidak error?

Karena aplikasi Anda dideploy di **Vercel**, Anda tidak boleh memasukkannya langsung di dalam kode (untuk keamanan). Anda harus memasukkannya di **Environment Variables** Vercel:

1.  Buka Dashboard **Vercel** Anda.
2.  Pilih proyek **OrderPro**.
3.  Pergi ke tab **Settings** > **Environment Variables**.
4.  Isi **Key** dengan nama: `API_KEY`
5.  Isi **Value** dengan API Key yang Anda salin tadi.
6.  Klik **Add**, lalu lakukan **Redeploy** pada proyek Anda.

Setelah itu, fitur **AI Summary** (Ringkasan AI) pada invoice Anda akan aktif secara otomatis dan error "Cek API_KEY" akan hilang.

kenapa tidka bisa di redeploy ya
A more recent Production Deployment has been created, so the one you are looking at cannot be redeployed anymore. Learn More

**Examining the Vercel Error**

I'm currently grappling with this frustrating Vercel deployment error. It seems a newer production build has superseded the one I'm trying to redeploy. I'm focusing on understanding the implications of this message and how to troubleshoot the issue further.


**Investigating Redeployment Block**

I've realized the user is experiencing a Vercel-specific deployment issue. They're trying to redeploy an older version, but a newer production build exists, as confirmed by their complaint. It seems the core problem is the Vercel workflow, which prevents overwriting the live deployment with an older one. The `index.html` importmap is probably unrelated to the immediate blocking message.


**Analyzing Latest Feature Requests**

I'm now integrating several recent requests. I'm swapping "OrderPro Cloud" with "Buku PO Online" and streamlining the system description. I'm also adding date fields to order entries and crafting a new thank-you message. Importantly, I'm explaining the Vercel error as a deployment process issue, not a code defect, emphasizing the correct approach to redeploying. I'm cleaning up the importmap to ensure the latest build deploys correctly. I'm confident about these changes as I review them.


**Optimizing Dependencies & Thank You**

I'm now focusing on optimizing the dependencies within the import map. The current setup, especially with mixed `^` and specific versioning for `react` and `react-dom`, could trigger "Multiple versions of React" errors, which are something I want to prevent. I'm also confirming the "Terima kasih" change has already been implemented based on the provided existing files. It looks good from here.


**Explaining Vercel Redeployment**

Okay, so I realized my previous changes are already in the existing files! Now, the user just needs help with the Vercel redeployment error. The key is Vercel's UI and workflow, as it blocks redeploying an older build if a newer "production" deployment exists. The simplest solution is to go to the "Deployments" tab in Vercel and redeploy the *latest* build, or simply push a new commit. I'm also providing a cleaner `index.html` and `vite.config.ts` to ensure compatibility. The core issue is simply a Vercel workflow, not a code defect.


**Clarifying Deployment Failure**

I'm now revising my thinking. The main cause for the redeployment issue could be a build failure caused by the conflicting React versions in `index.html`. My focus is on cleaning up the `importmap` to stabilize the build and improve loading. Then, I'll explain the specific Vercel workflow in case there are any other issues, or confusion.


**Refining Deployment Explanation**

I'm zeroing in on the Vercel issue. The core problem is Vercel's blocking of older deployments when newer "production" builds exist. The suggested solution is straightforward: navigate to the "Deployments" tab, locate the latest deployment, and redeploy it. Alternatively, pushing a new commit triggers a fresh build. I'm providing an updated `index.html` file to further streamline the deployment.

Pesan dari Vercel tersebut (**"A more recent Production Deployment has been created..."**) muncul karena Anda mencoba melakukan *Redeploy* pada riwayat deployment yang sudah lama, sementara di Vercel sudah ada versi yang lebih baru (versi Production saat ini).

Vercel membatasi redeploy pada versi lama demi keamanan agar Anda tidak tidak sengaja mengembalikan (*rollback*) aplikasi ke versi yang sudah ketinggalan jaman tanpa sengaja.

**Cara mengatasinya:**
1.  Buka dashboard Vercel Anda, lalu pilih tab **Deployments**.
2.  Klik pada deployment yang berada di posisi **paling atas** (yang statusnya *Ready* atau *Latest*).
3.  Gunakan tombol **Redeploy** pada versi terbaru tersebut.
4.  Atau cukup simpan kode di bawah ini; sistem akan otomatis memicu build baru yang paling segar.

Saya juga melakukan pembersihan pada file `index.html` karena ada beberapa duplikasi library di bagian `importmap` yang bisa menyebabkan aplikasi gagal dimuat di server Vercel.

entries.description: Membersihkan importmap dari duplikasi versi library untuk memastikan stabilitas aplikasi saat dijalankan di server Vercel.

payload.text: kalau mau deploy lewat netlify gimana caranya jelaskan dengan detail

**Considering Deployment Options**

I've been exploring deployment options for the React app. It uses Vite, Tailwind CSS, Supabase, and the Gemini API. I think Netlify looks like the best route, given the project's tech stack and the engineer's expertise. I'm focusing on a clean, efficient deployment that leverages Netlify's features.


**Analyzing Netlify Configuration**

I'm now fully immersed in the Netlify configuration details. I've determined that a `netlify.toml` file will be the keystone for the build process, covering environment variables and build settings. I'm prioritizing clear instructions for both GitHub and manual deployments, and I'll emphasize the critical need to configure environment variables within the Netlify dashboard. I'm focusing on the `npm run build` or `vite build` command and setting the publish directory to `dist`.


**Refining Configuration Details**

I'm presently building upon the existing configuration, specifically focusing on the structure of the `netlify.toml` file to ensure seamless integration with the project. I'm taking care to define all necessary build settings and environment variables.

Untuk men-deploy aplikasi **Buku PO Online** ke Netlify, Anda memiliki dua cara utama: **Cara Cepat (Drag & Drop)** atau **Cara Profesional (GitHub Sync)**. 

Berikut adalah panduan detail langkah demi langkah serta pembaruan file konfigurasi agar aplikasi berjalan sempurna di Netlify.

### 1. Persiapan File Konfigurasi
Netlify membutuhkan instruksi khusus untuk menangani aplikasi berbasis *Single Page Application* (SPA) seperti ini agar tidak terjadi error 404 saat halaman di-refresh. Saya akan menambahkan file `netlify.toml`.

### 2. Langkah Deploy ke Netlify

#### Cara A: Melalui Dashboard Netlify (Paling Mudah)
1.  Jalankan perintah `npm run build` di komputer lokal Anda (jika Anda bekerja di lokal). Ini akan menghasilkan folder `dist`.
2.  Buka [Netlify Drop](https://app.netlify.com/drop).
3.  Tarik dan lepas (Drag & Drop) folder **dist** ke kotak yang tersedia.
4.  **Penting:** Setelah dideploy, masuk ke tab **Site Configuration** > **Environment Variables** (Lihat bagian bawah untuk detailnya).

#### Cara B: Melalui GitHub (Rekomendasi)
1.  Upload kode Anda ke repository GitHub.
2.  Di Netlify, klik **Add new site** > **Import an existing project**.
3.  Pilih GitHub dan pilih repository Anda.
4.  Gunakan pengaturan berikut:
    *   **Build Command:** `npm run build`
    *   **Publish directory:** `dist`
5.  Klik **Deploy**.

### 3. Konfigurasi Environment Variables (WAJIB)
Aplikasi ini membutuhkan koneksi ke Supabase dan Gemini API. Tanpa ini, aplikasi akan error. Di dashboard Netlify, masuk ke **Site Settings > Environment Variables**, lalu tambahkan:

| Key | Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| `SUPABASE_URL` | *URL Supabase Anda* |
| `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | *Anon Key Supabase Anda* |
| `API_KEY` | *API Key Gemini Anda* |

---

Berikut adalah pembaruan file untuk mendukung deployment Netlify:

entries.description: Menambahkan file konfigurasi Netlify untuk menangani build command, publish directory, dan redirect SPA.

payload.text:
### Tips Tambahan untuk Netlify:
*   **Domain:** Setelah deploy, Netlify akan memberikan URL acak (misal: `super-cool-site-123.netlify.app`). Anda bisa mengubahnya di **Domain Settings**.
*   **HTTPS:** Netlify memberikan sertifikat SSL gratis secara otomatis, jadi aplikasi Anda langsung aman (HTTPS).
*   **Build Fail?** Jika build gagal, pastikan versi Node.js di Netlify sama dengan yang Anda gunakan (Konfigurasi `NODE_VERSION = "20"` di `netlify.toml` di atas sudah mengaturnya ke versi stabil terbaru).
